PhD Workload Allocation

Source: vignettes/e-phd-workload.Rmd
e-phd-workload.Rmd

Model introduction

Consider a situation where a department needs to allocate semester workload across PhD students. Let there be NsN_s students indexed by i∈{1,…,Ns}i \in \{1,\ldots,N_s\} and NjN_j courses indexed by j∈{1,…,Nj}j \in \{1,\ldots,N_j\}. For each course-role pair, where r∈{TA,GR,E}r \in \{\mathrm{TA}, \mathrm{GR}, \mathrm{E}\}, the required demand dj,rd_{j,r} must be fully assigned.

For each student-course pair, Pi,jP_{i,j} denotes the TA preference score. Student seniority is represented by sis_i, where Year-1, Year-2, Year-3 and Year-4 are mapped to −1,0,1,2-1,0,1,2 respectively. We also track prior-semester TA and GR workload, denoted by ti(1)t_i^{(1)} and gi(1)g_i^{(1)}.

This model allocates current-semester TA, GR and E units while balancing:

  1. TA fairness across non-Year-1 students,
  2. TA preference satisfaction,
  3. Seniority-aware E allocation, and
  4. protection of Year-1 students from excessive TA load.

Objective function

The model minimises the weighted objective:

Xi,j,r∈ℤ≥0units of role r in course j assigned to student iti(2)=∑j=1NjXi,j,TAcurrent-semester TA workloadgi(2)=∑j=1NjXi,j,GRcurrent-semester GR workloadei(2)=∑j=1NjXi,j,Ecurrent-semester E workloadTi=ti(1)+ti(2)yearly TA workloadGi=gi(1)+gi(2)yearly GR workloadwi≥0slack for Year-1 TA soft boundTmax≥0maximum yearly TA workload among students with si≥0Tmin≥0minimum yearly TA workload among students with si≥0\begin{align*} X_{i,j,r} &\in \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0} && \text{units of role } r \text{ in course } j \text{ assigned to student } i \\ t_i^{(2)} &= \sum_{j=1}^{N_j} X_{i,j,\mathrm{TA}} && \text{current-semester TA workload} \\ g_i^{(2)} &= \sum_{j=1}^{N_j} X_{i,j,\mathrm{GR}} && \text{current-semester GR workload} \\ e_i^{(2)} &= \sum_{j=1}^{N_j} X_{i,j,\mathrm{E}} && \text{current-semester E workload} \\ T_i &= t_i^{(1)} + t_i^{(2)} && \text{yearly TA workload} \\ G_i &= g_i^{(1)} + g_i^{(2)} && \text{yearly GR workload} \\ w_i &\ge 0 && \text{slack for Year-1 TA soft bound} \\ T_{\max} &\ge 0 && \text{maximum yearly TA workload among students with } s_i \ge 0 \\ T_{\min} &\ge 0 && \text{minimum yearly TA workload among students with } s_i \ge 0 \end{align*}

min⁡α(Tmax−Tmin)−β∑i=1Ns∑j=1NjPi,jXi,j,TA−ϕ∑i=1Ns∑j=1NjsiXi,j,E+ρ∑i:si=−1wi \min \quad \alpha (T_{\max} - T_{\min}) - \beta \sum_{i=1}^{N_s} \sum_{j=1}^{N_j} P_{i,j} X_{i,j,\mathrm{TA}} - \phi \sum_{i=1}^{N_s} \sum_{j=1}^{N_j} s_i X_{i,j,\mathrm{E}} + \rho \sum_{i:s_i=-1} w_i

where α,β,ϕ,ρ≥0\alpha,\beta,\phi,\rho \ge 0 are user-specified weights.

Constraints

Demand satisfaction

For every course and role, assigned units must match demand:

∑i=1NsXi,j,r=dj,r,∀j,r∈{TA,GR,E} \sum_{i=1}^{N_s} X_{i,j,r} = d_{j,r}, \quad \forall j,\; r \in \{\mathrm{TA}, \mathrm{GR}, \mathrm{E}\}

TA spread among non-Year-1 students

The spread term applies only to students with si≥0s_i \ge 0:

Ti≤Tmax,∀i:si≥0Ti≥Tmin,∀i:si≥0\begin{align} T_i &\le T_{\max}, \quad \forall i : s_i \ge 0 \\ T_i &\ge T_{\min}, \quad \forall i : s_i \ge 0 \end{align}

Annual workload equality

Let CC denote semester workload capacity per student. The model fixes each student’s annual workload total at 2C2C.

Ti+Gi+ei(2)=2C,∀i T_i + G_i + e_i^{(2)} = 2C, \quad \forall i

Year-1 TA soft upper bound

For Year-1 students, current-semester TA load is softly capped:

ti(2)≤tmax⁡(Y1)+wi,∀i:si=−1 t_i^{(2)} \le t_{\max}^{(Y1)} + w_i, \quad \forall i : s_i = -1

Optional current-semester workload bounds

If provided by the user, the following bounds are imposed:

tmin⁡(2)≤ti(2)≤tmax⁡(2),∀igmin⁡(2)≤gi(2)≤gmax⁡(2),∀iemin⁡(2)≤ei(2)≤emax⁡(2),∀i\begin{align} t_{\min}^{(2)} \le t_i^{(2)} \le t_{\max}^{(2)}, \quad \forall i \\ g_{\min}^{(2)} \le g_i^{(2)} \le g_{\max}^{(2)}, \quad \forall i \\ e_{\min}^{(2)} \le e_i^{(2)} \le e_{\max}^{(2)}, \quad \forall i \end{align}

If any bound parameter is omitted, the corresponding constraint is not added.
